executive_summary: Latest updates from 2 sources covering the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Monitoring 2 key developments. AI analysis refreshing shortly.
execSummaryRich: Escalation: HIGH. Key developments: Kuwait Petroleum Corporation says Iranian hit causes 'severe' damage to units. Oman and Iran hold meeting on Strait of Hormuz. Situation remains active.

key_developments.headline: Kuwait Petroleum Corporation says Iranian hit causes 'severe' damage to units
key_developments.detail: Kuwait Petroleum Corporation reported "severe material damage" on Sunday, following an Iranian drone attack which set ablaze operating units. No injuries were reported, the statement added, noting that teams are working to keep the fires under control and to prevent them spreading to other facilitie

key_developments.headline: Oman and Iran hold meeting on Strait of Hormuz
key_developments.detail: Oman and Iran held talks on easing passage through the Strait of Hormuz, the Omani state news agency reported on Sunday. terminology_explained.simple_explanation: Iran's elite Revolutionary Guard — controls missiles and drones.
Narrow waterway — 20% of world oil passes through it.

what_to_watch_next: Monitor live feeds for breaking developments.

india_impact.headline: India Monitors Gulf Conflict
india_impact.detail: India's energy security tied to Gulf stability.
india_impact.full_detail: India imports 85% of crude oil from Gulf. Strait of Hormuz disruption would immediately impact supply.

indiaSummary: India continues to monitor the US-Israel-Iran conflict closely given its significant dependence on Gulf energy imports and the large Indian diaspora. Oil price volatility directly impacts petrol prices for Indian consumers.
